title: Supplement Safety and Medication Interaction Checklist
description: Use this checklist before starting any supplement to ensure safety, proper dosing, and compatibility with your current medications. This course emphasizes that supplements should always be discussed with your healthcare provider.
instructions: Before starting any new supplement, review each safety item. Check off each step you've completed. Do NOT start supplements without checking medication interactions.

items.label: I have discussed this supplement with my healthcare provider
I have checked for interactions with my current medications
If I take an SSRI/SNRI: I have confirmed this supplement does NOT risk serotonin syndrome (avoid 5-HTP, St. John's Wort, L-tryptophan)
If I take an MAOI: I understand tyramine dietary restrictions and have reviewed supplement compatibility
If I take lithium: I understand sodium, magnesium, and NSAID interactions that affect lithium levels
The supplement has third-party testing (USP, NSF, or ConsumerLab certification)
I am starting with the lowest recommended dose and will titrate up slowly
I am introducing only one new supplement at a time (wait 2 weeks between additions)
I know the proper timing for this supplement (with meals, away from medications, etc.)
I have a plan to monitor for effects and side effects over the next 4-8 weeks
I have baseline lab work if applicable (vitamin D, B12, magnesium, iron, etc.)
My reason for considering this supplement:
items.placeholder: e.g., To support sleep quality based on research showing magnesium improves insomnia

disclaimer: This checklist is for educational purposes only. NEVER start, stop, or change supplements without consulting your healthcare provider, especially if you take psychiatric medications. Some supplement-medication combinations can be dangerous or life-threatening (e.g., serotonin syndrome). If you are in crisis, call or text 988.
